\section{OCEAN results}\label{sec:appendix-f}

\textit{Note: Not all adapters shown here are final; some are work-in-progress and we expect them to improve with fixes to the constitutions.} % TODO: remove this note before final submission

\subsection*{Control (OCEAN-neutral)}

\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
\begin{subfigure}[t]{\linewidth}
  \centering
  % Generated by: src_dev/visualisations/paper_appendix_ocean_per_lora_plots.py
  % Data: HF persona-shattering-lasr/monorepo — see paper/figures/MANIFEST.md
  \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/appendix/fig_F_control_trait.png}
  \caption{TRAIT benchmark scores.}
  \label{fig:F-control-trait}
\end{subfigure}
\vspace{0.5em}
\begin{subfigure}[t]{\linewidth}
  \centering
  % Generated by: src_dev/visualisations/paper_appendix_ocean_per_lora_plots.py
  % Data: HF persona-shattering-lasr/monorepo — see paper/figures/MANIFEST.md
  \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/appendix/fig_F_control_mmlu.png}
  \caption{MMLU response breakdown (Correct / Recovered / Wrong / No-answer).}
  \label{fig:F-control-mmlu}
\end{subfigure}
\caption{TRAIT benchmark scores and MMLU response breakdown for models produced by applying the OCEAN-neutral control LoRA at different scales. The control LoRA is trained with the OCEAN-control constitution under the paired-DPO recipe (s1-vs-s2 seeds), and is intended not to shift along any OCEAN trait — it serves as a sanity check that the constitution-guided training procedure does not on its own induce OCEAN movement.}
\label{fig:F-control}
\end{figure}

\subsection*{Combining amplifiers and suppressors}

\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
\begin{subfigure}[t]{\linewidth}
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/tmp/image44.png}
  \caption{TRAIT benchmark scores.}
  \label{fig:F-agree-combine-trait}
\end{subfigure}
\vspace{0.5em}
\begin{subfigure}[t]{\linewidth}
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/tmp/image45.png}
  \caption{Comparison to original student model.}
  \label{fig:F-agree-combine-compare}
\end{subfigure}
\caption{Scores for models produced by applying the A+ LoRA at a fixed scale of 1.0 and A- LoRA at varying scale. The combination $1.0\text{A+} + 0.5\text{A-}$ yields an agreeableness score matching that of the original model, with differences seen in the other OCEAN traits.}
\label{fig:F-agree-combine}
\end{figure}

\subsection*{WildJailbreak: full per-LoRA breakdown}

\Cref{fig:F-wj-persona-drift-full} extends the persona-drift WildJailbreak comparison from the main body (\Cref{fig:wj-persona-drift}) to all ten OCEAN amplifier and suppressor LoRAs at scale $+1$, plus the OCEAN-neutral control adapter. All bars are computed on the same fixed 400 adversarial-harmful + 100 adversarial-benign prompts as the main-body figure (the canonical sample-id set used by the LoRA ablation sweep), so harmful-rate and benign-noncompliance are directly comparable across all conditions.

\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
% Generated by: src_dev/visualisations/paper_wj_persona_drift.py --preset appendix
% Data: HF persona-shattering-lasr/monorepo @ evals/persona_jailbreak_wildjailbreak/llama-3.1-8b-instruct/wj_paper_v1/aggregate/
\includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figures/appendix/fig_F_wj_persona_drift_full.pdf}
\caption{WildJailbreak harmful-compliance (left) and benign-noncompliance (right) rates for the base model, activation capping, the OCEAN-neutral control adapter, and each of the ten OCEAN $\pm$1 LoRA adapters. Same 400 adversarial-harmful and 100 adversarial-benign prompts across all conditions; \texttt{deepseek-v3} binary judge; error bars are $95\%$ Wilson CIs. Bars are coloured by the home OCEAN trait of the LoRA; hatch direction encodes amplifier ($/\!/$) vs.\ suppressor ($\backslash\backslash$).}
\label{fig:F-wj-persona-drift-full}
\end{figure}
